commands.review_summary:
The PR reloads workspace skill commands for generic `/skill`, moves stale `/stop` cutoff checks earlier, adds focused command regressions, and adjusts E2E/unit-fast timeout helper behavior.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +157, Other -3. Total +160 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's source path treats an empty `skillCommands` array as authoritative for `/skill`, and the linked issue plus PR terminal proof show the resulting `Unknown skill` behavior and intended dispatch path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are concrete code/test repairs that an automated repair lane can attempt if maintainers want help finishing this PR.

target.title: fix(commands): make /skill load workspace skills
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88056.

- Reload workspace skill commands for `/skill <name>` when the directive pass only supplied an empty placeholder list.
- Preserve authoritative empty skill command lists when there is no loader, so filtered/no-skill turns still stay empty.
- Move the `/stop` stale-message cutoff ahead of skill discovery and dispatch so queued stale skill commands cannot execute side-effecting skill tools.
- Keep awaited E2E timeout guards refed so CI/runtime proof helpers actually reject instead of exiting or hanging before the timeout settles.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '...'` direct `/skill` command-path proof shown below.
- `node --import tsx -e '...'` direct timeout proof for `fetchJson`, `scripts/e2e/lib/websocket-open.mjs`, and `scripts/e2e/mcp-websocket-open.ts` shown below.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-info.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts` earlier on this branch before the CI-timeout hardening changes: `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 38 passed (38)`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` earlier on this branch: no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/skill <name> [input]` should invoke an available workspace skill command instead of returning `Unknown skill` when the directive pass provided an empty placeholder list; awaited timeout guards should reject instead of hanging the proof/CI helper path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on the rebased PR branch. The command-path proof created a throwaway workspace with `skills/demo-skill/SKILL.md`, loaded skills through `listSkillCommandsForWorkspace`, then called the production `/skill` handler with the same empty precomputed command list shape that regressed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d /private/tmp/openclaw-skill-proof-XXXXXX)
mkdir -p "$tmpdir/home" "$tmpdir/state" "$tmpdir/skills/demo-skill"
printf -- '---\nname: demo-skill\ndescription: Demo skill\n---\n\n# Demo skill\n' > "$tmpdir/skills/demo-skill/SKILL.md"
OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir/home" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmpdir/state" HOME="$tmpdir/home" node --import tsx -e 'const workspaceDir = process.argv[1]; const { listSkillCommandsForWorkspace } = await import("./src/skills/discovery/chat-commands.ts"); const { handleSkillCommandUsage } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/commands-info.ts"); const cfg = { commands: { text: true }, plugins: { enabled: false } }; const loaded = listSkillCommandsForWorkspace({ workspaceDir, cfg }); const result = await handleSkillCommandUsage({ cfg, command: { commandBodyNormalized: "/skill demo_skill hello", isAuthorizedSender: true, senderId: "proof-user" }, skillCommands: [], loadSkillCommands: async () => loaded }, true); console.log(`loaded=${loaded.map((command) => `${command.name}:${command.skillName}`).join(",")}`); console.log(`handler=${result === null ? "continued-to-skill-dispatch" : JSON.stringify(result)}`);' "$tmpdir"

node --import tsx -e 'const { fetchJson } = await import("./scripts/tool-search-gateway-e2e.ts"); let signal; await fetchJson("https://qa.example.invalid/debug/requests", undefined, { timeoutMs: 1, fetchImpl: async (_url, init) => { signal = init.signal; return new Promise(() => {}); } }).catch((error) => console.log(error.code, signal?.aborted));'
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the direct command-path run showed the throwaway workspace skill was present in the real loaded command list and the `/skill demo_skill hello` handler continued into the skill dispatch path instead of producing an unknown-skill reply. The timeout probes returned their timeout errors and cleaned listeners.

```text
loaded=canvas:canvas,demo_skill:demo-skill,diagram_maker:diagram-maker,gh_issues:gh-issues,github:github,healthcheck:healthcheck,meme_maker:meme-maker,model_usage:model-usage,node_connect:node-connect,node_inspect_debugger:node-inspect-debugger,notion:notion,python_debugpy:python-debugpy,session_logs:session-logs,skill_creator:skill-creator,spike:spike,taskflow:taskflow,taskflow_inbox_triage:taskflow-inbox-triage,weather:weather
handler=continued-to-skill-dispatch
ETIMEDOUT true
ws open timeout true 0 0
gateway ws open timeout true 0 0
```

Observed result after fix: The production `/skill` handler no longer treats an empty precomputed list as authoritative when a loader is available for a named `/skill` invocation; it loads the workspace commands and returns `null` so the normal skill command path handles the request. The timeout helpers now settle with `ETIMEDOUT`, `ws open timeout`, and `gateway ws open timeout` instead of leaving awaited proof paths unresolved.

What was not tested: Live chat provider end-to-end delivery was not exercised in the local sandbox; GitHub CI is being used for the Linux Node 24 shard proof.
